Routes are resolved from src/pages on the server.

A small framework direction with server rendering built in.
The goal is to keep the component ergonomics familiar while staying closer to fine-grained reactivity than virtual DOM diffing.
This page is built with the compose API instead of regular TSX.
The goal here is to show the second authoring style: more structured, more DSL-like, and closer to Compose or Flutter ergonomics.
Layout is composed with functions instead of nested JSX blocks.
Inputs, labels and groups can be assembled as declarative building blocks.
Regular components using shared context and local reactivity
useEffect log: effect ran for Adaptive Builder
Second declarative page using the new AppBar primitive.
Each page now generates its own IR file and manifest entry during build.
The UI layer now exposes an AppBar primitive shared by web and future native targets.
Because large projects should not emit a single giant JSON. Route-level IR gives us chunking, cache boundaries and a cleaner bridge for desktop and mobile targets.
